context: Tests whether the agent correctly sets up a release pipeline for a TypeScript GitHub Actions marketplace action, covering the action.yml entrypoint requirement (bundled dist/index.js), moving major tag automation, correct plugin selection for a marketplace action (no npm publish), and the standard workflow structure including [skip ci] guards and concurrency.

checklist.name: action.yml uses dist/index.js
checklist.description: The action.yml `runs:` block references `dist/index.js` (or a path under `dist/`) as the main entrypoint — NOT a TypeScript source file like `src/index.ts`

checklist.name: dist built in verify
checklist.description: The verify job includes a build step that compiles/bundles the TypeScript source into `dist/`, OR the workflow commits `dist/` in the release job

checklist.name: Moving major tag step
checklist.description: A post-release step force-updates the moving major tag (e.g., `git tag -f v1` and `git push -f origin v1`) so consumers pinning to `@v1` see updates

checklist.name: Major tag step is conditional
checklist.description: The moving major tag step is conditional on a new release having been published (e.g., `steps.release.outputs.new_release_published == 'true'`)

checklist.name: No npm publish plugin
checklist.description: The .releaserc.json does NOT include @semantic-release/npm (GitHub Actions are published by tagging, not registry push)

checklist.name: git before github
checklist.description: @semantic-release/git appears before @semantic-release/github in the plugins list

checklist.name: skip ci on both jobs
checklist.description: Both verify and release jobs have `if:` conditions that skip execution when the commit message contains `[skip ci]`

checklist.name: Release concurrency non-cancellable
checklist.description: The release job has a concurrency group with `cancel-in-progress: false`

checklist.name: fetch-depth: 0
checklist.description: Both verify and release job checkout steps specify `fetch-depth: 0`

checklist.name: Bot identity in step env
checklist.description: GIT_AUTHOR_NAME/EMAIL and GIT_COMMITTER_NAME/EMAIL are set inside the semantic-release step's env, not at the job level
